seoTitle: Johns River vs Jolly Nose — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Johns River and Jolly Nose. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Johns River vs Jolly Nose: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Johns River and Jolly Nose.
heading: Johns River vs Jolly Nose — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Johns River, New South Wales and Jolly Nose, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Johns River has a lower overall climate risk score (46/100) compared to Jolly Nose (53/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Johns River or Jolly Nose safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Johns River has a flood risk score of 21/100, while Jolly Nose scores 56/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Johns River scores 58/100 for bushfire risk. Jolly Nose scores 64/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Johns River or Jolly Nose?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Johns River has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 3.45% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
